name: Valadouro – tour through Alfoz and Rio Ouro
name_original: Valadouro – vuelta por Alfoz y río Ouro
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around O Valadouro traverse a diverse landscape in Galicia, Spain. The region features varied terrain, including river valleys, coastal areas, and forests, offering a range of cycling challenges. Routes often include significant elevation gains, with some segments on unpaved surfaces. Natural features like the Ouro River valley and proximity to the Cantabrian coast define the area's cycling experience. Best touring cycling routes around O Valadouro The most popular touring cycling route is Burela - loop via Covo river and Marosa Beach, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route integrates river and beach landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Valadouro – tour through Alfoz and Rio Ouro, a difficult 19.9 miles (32.1 km) path. This route traverses the Ouro River valley with notable elevation changes. Local touring cyclists also love the Foz Promenade – A Rapadoira Beach loop from Nois, a 30.7 miles (49.4 km) trail leading through coastal paths and beaches, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Touring cycling around O Valadouro is defined by river valleys, coastal paths, and forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to challenging. The routes in O Valadouro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore O Valadouro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around O Valadouro?
A: There are nearly 40 touring cycling routes available around O Valadouro. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, including river valleys, coastal areas, and forests.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in O Valadouro?
A: The touring cycling routes in O Valadouro are generally moderate to difficult. There are 13 moderate routes and 26 difficult routes, often featuring significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018486123/valadouro-tour-through-alfoz-and-rio-ouro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valadouro – tour through Alfoz and Rio Ouro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult path with over 700 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in O Valadouro?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around O Valadouro are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017423035/burela-loop-via-covo-river-and-marosa-beach" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burela - loop via Covo river and Marosa Beach&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz Promenade – A Rapadoira Beach loop from Nois&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that feature natural landmarks like waterfalls or unique forests?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to impressive natural features. You can find routes near waterfalls like Fervenza do Escouridal and Cascada del Pozo da Onza. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6453869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Souto da Retorta (Retorta Grove)&lt;/a&gt;, a natural monument with colossal eucalyptus trees, offers a unique and practically flat path for cycling or walking.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that offer coastal views or access to beaches?
A: Yes, O Valadouro's proximity to the Cantabrian coast means several routes incorporate stunning coastal scenery and beaches. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3990390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Rapadoira Beach – Foz Promenade loop from As Teixoeiras&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you along coastal paths and past &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2927996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Rapadoira Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore along the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5128781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Walkway of Foz&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views, and the charming town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5345593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz&lt;/a&gt; with its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;harbor&lt;/a&gt; is a great stop. You might also encounter historical sites like the Castle of Castrodouro or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4560734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Abuelo Eucalyptus Tree&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in O Valadouro?
A: The touring cycling routes in O Valadouro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Over 300 touring cyclists have explored the area, often praising the diverse terrain that integrates river valleys, coastal areas, and forests, offering a varied and engaging experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easier touring cycling options in O Valadouro?
A: While many routes in O Valadouro are moderate to difficult, some segments, like the path through &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6453869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Souto da Retorta&lt;/a&gt;, are practically flat and suitable for a more relaxed pace. When planning, look for routes with lower elevation gains and shorter distances for a more family-friendly experience.

Q: What kind of surfaces can I expect on the cycling routes?
A: The routes around O Valadouro feature a mix of surfaces. While many segments are paved, offering a smooth ride, some parts may be unpaved, providing a more adventurous off-road experience. It's advisable to check individual route details for specific surface information.

Q: Is it possible to access the famous Beach of the Cathedrals (Praia das Catedrais) by bike from O Valadouro?
A: O Valadouro is located in the Mariña Lucense, making the famous Beach of the Cathedrals a short drive away. While direct cycling access to the beach itself might be restricted or challenging, you can certainly plan a cycling route in the vicinity and then visit the beach separately. Always check local regulations for beach access.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marosa Beach – Covo River Bridge loop from Burela&lt;/a&gt; cover a significant distance of nearly 72 km with over 1100 meters of elevation gain, making it a challenging option for experienced touring cyclists.
tips: A small local church where every August the "Rapa das Bestas" festival of São Tomé is celebrated, with wild horses running down from the Sierra del Xistral to the valley.

The Rio Ouro nature reserve is part of the Natura 2000 network. It starts at about 800 m on the eastern slope of the Sierra do Xistral and flows into the Atlantic at Foz. The Escouridal waterfall, not far from the LU-P6306 where the Guilfonso river rushes against the granite rock from a height of 80 m, is worth seeing if you have time.

The stone castle was built on top of an ancient Roman fortress from the 1st century AD. C., of which only the structure of a tower remains. The castle was converted into a monastery in the 9th century. Great views of the surrounding landscape from the top of the hill.

waypoints.name: Church of Santo Tomé de Recaré
